title: Update $text Queries with MongoDB Search for Improved Search Performance

sections.heading: MongoDB Search Feature Advantages
sections.body:
The [$search](pipe.$search) aggregation stage provides the following
features which are either not available through the [$text](manual/reference/operator/query/text)
operator, available but less performant, or available only with
significant implementation work by the user:

- [Language awareness](ref-language-analyzers)

- Case-insensitive and diacritic-insensitive search

- Result text [highlights](highlight-ref)

- Geospatial-aware queries with [geoWithin](geowithin-ref) and
  [geoShape](geoshape-ref).

- Character and word autocompletion with different
  [tokenization](bson-data-types-autocomplete) strategies

- [Fuzzy matching](text-ref-fuzzy-eg)

- Filtering on 10 or more strings with the [compound](compound-ref) operator

- Customizable [relevance scoring](scoring-ref) and
  [sorting](sort-ref)

- Single compound indexes on arrays

- [Synonym](synonyms-ref) search

- Bucketing for [faceted navigation](fts-facet-ref)

- [Custom analyzers](custom-analyzers)

- [Phrase](phrase-ref) queries

sections.heading: Create the Indexes
sections.body:
The examples in the following sections use queries against the
`sample_mflix.movies` collection in the
sample data to illustrate the improvements to
flexibility and performance that MongoDB Search offers over [$text](manual/reference/operator/query/text). You
can run the queries from both examples using the following indexes:

| Text Index                                                           | MongoDB Search Index                                                                                                                                 |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| db.movies.createIndex( \{ genres: "text", plot: "text", year: -1 } ) | \{ "mappings": \{ "dynamic": false, "fields": \{ "genres": \{ "type": "string" }, "plot": \{ "type": "string" }, "year": \{ "type": "number" } } } } |

Either index definition will index the `genres` and `plot` fields
as text, and the `year` field as numeric. For instructions on
creating [$text](manual/reference/operator/query/text) indexes, see
[Create Text Index](manual/core/indexes/index-types/index-text/create-text-index).
For instructions on creating MongoDB Search indexes, see
[create a MongoDB Search index](ref-create-index).

sections.heading: Improve Flexibility of Full-Text Queries with MongoDB Search
Improve Performance of Queries using MongoDB Search
sections.body:
In addition to greater flexibility and convenience, MongoDB Search provides
significant performance advantages over analogous [$text](manual/reference/operator/query/text) queries.
Consider a query against the `sample_mflix.movies` collection to
retrieve movies released between 2000 and 2010, in the comedy genre,
with 'poet' in the `plot` field.

Run the following queries:

| Text Index                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | MongoDB Search Index                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| db.movies.aggregate(\[ \{ $match: \{ year: \{$gte: 2000, $lte: 2010}, $text: \{ $search: "poet" }, genres : \{ $eq: "Comedy" } } }, \{ "$sort": \{ "year": 1 } }, \{ "$limit": 3 }, \{ "$project": \{ "\_id": 0, "title": 1, "genres": 1, "plot": 1, "year": 1 }, } ]) | db.movies.aggregate(\[ \{ "$search": \{ "compound": \{ "filter": \[\{ "range": \{ "gte": 2000, "lte": 2010, "path": "year" } }, \{ "text": \{ "path": "plot", "query": "poet" } }, \{ "text": \{ "path": "genres", "query": "comedy" } }] } } }, \{ "$sort": \{ "year": 1 } }, \{ "$limit": 3 }, \{ "$project": \{ "\_id": 0, "title": 1, "genres": 1, "plot": 1, "year": 1 } } ]) |

Both of these queries will return the following three documents.

```javascript
   {
  year: 2000,
  plot: 'A film poem inspired by the Peruvian poet Cèsar Vallejo. A story about our need for love, our confusion, greatness and smallness and, most of all, our vulnerability. It is a story with many...',
  genres: [ 'Comedy', 'Drama' ],
  title: 'Songs from the Second Floor'
},
{
  plot: 'When his mother, who has sheltered him his entire 40 years, dies, Elling, a sensitive, would-be poet, is sent to live in a state institution. There he meets Kjell Bjarne, a gentle giant and...',
  genres: [ 'Comedy', 'Drama' ],
  title: 'Elling',
  year: 2001
},
{
  plot: 'Heart-broken after several affairs, a woman finds herself torn between a Poet and a TV Host.',
  genres: [ 'Comedy', 'Romance', 'Drama' ],
  title: 'Easy',
  year: 2003
}
```

Although [$text](manual/reference/operator/query/text) is adequate for simple, narrow searches such as
this, as the size of the datasets and breadth of your queries
increases, the performance advantages of `$search` will significantly
improve the responsiveness of your applications. We recommend that you
use a [Queries and Indexes](searching-ref) through the
[$search](pipe.$search) aggregation pipeline stage.

sections.heading: Learn More
sections.body:
- To learn more about MongoDB Search queries, see [Queries and Indexes](searching-ref).

- MongoDB University offers a free course on optimizing MongoDB
  Performance. To learn more, see
  [Monitoring and Insights](https://learn.mongodb.com/courses/monitoring-and-insights).
